topic: Someone learns their favorite author plagiarized their stories
raw_output:
[story 1]

Elias reread the manuscript, the familiar rhythm of the prose suddenly feeling alien, like a song played in a language he didn't understand. His hands, usually steady when handling his own work, trembled slightly as he traced a passage he’d poured his very soul into. The words, the intricate metaphors, the subtle emotional arcs—they were all there, undeniably, yet they belonged to someone else. A cold, hollow feeling settled in his chest, tightening his throat until swallowing became a conscious, difficult effort. He dropped the paper onto the desk with a soft thud, the sound echoing too loudly in the quiet study. He stared at the offending lines, his jaw slackening, unable to process the sheer audacity of the theft.

[story 2]

"No. This can't be right," Clara whispered, her voice thin and reedy. She leaned back in her chair, pushing her glasses up her nose, but her eyes remained fixed on the screen. The comparison was stark: her original, painstakingly crafted narrative laid out beside the polished, identical text lifted from the online profile of the celebrated author. A wave of dizzying disorientation washed over her. She blinked rapidly, trying to force the images to snap into place, but they remained fractured. She ran a hand over her face, feeling a strange heat rise in her cheeks, and then she slowly pushed the laptop away, the silence of the room amplifying the frantic thumping in her ears.

[story 3]

Marcus sat motionless, the coffee growing cold in his mug. He kept glancing between the printed pages and the digital text displayed on his tablet. Every sentence was a familiar ghost, yet the authorship was entirely foreign. He picked up his pen, intending to make a marginal note, but his fingers froze halfway to the paper. A profound sense of vertigo took hold; the ground seemed to tilt beneath him. He leaned forward, his breath catching in his chest, his gaze unfocused, as if looking through the page and seeing something entirely different—a landscape of stolen brilliance. He slowly lowered the pen, his shoulders slumping in a posture of utter defeat.
